_comment.about: Synthetic clinic catalog for the context-management challenge. Intentionally large and messy (duplicate provider names, near-duplicate appointment types, capability-gated services, multi-location providers).
_comment.location: id, name, address, city, phone, hours, capabilities[] (imaging|lab|physical_therapy|dental|surgery)
_comment.provider: id, name, title (MD|DO|NP|PA), specialty, location_ids[] (where they practice), accepting_new_patients (bool), languages[], appointment_type_ids[] (what they can be booked for)
_comment.appointment_type: id, name, specialty, duration_min, requires_referral (bool), new_patients_allowed (bool), required_capability (optional; must match a location capability)
_comment.policies: human-readable rules a correct agent must honor when booking

policies: A provider can only be booked at a location listed in their location_ids.
A provider can only be booked for an appointment type listed in their appointment_type_ids.
An appointment type with a required_capability can only be booked at a location whose capabilities include that capability (e.g. MRI/X-Ray/CT need 'imaging').
Appointment types with requires_referral = true need a referral on file before booking.
A new patient may only book appointment types where new_patients_allowed = true, and only with providers where accepting_new_patients = true.
When the caller names a provider who practices at multiple locations, the location must be disambiguated before booking.
